programmes.show_description: Mens Sana: Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star in the top-rated crime drama series. Pascoe investigates when a woman is found dead at a private health club. Dalziel books in!
You Pays Yer Money: Sharon isn't registered to vote in Chigwell, but when election fever hits the constituency, she wants to make use of Darryl's vote.
The Children of the Battlefield: Corcoran closes in on those responsible for the kidnapping and murder of young Five Points men.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Trust: Tracey falls for her smooth therapist Gerald and argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship when he bans her from continuing her sessions.
Hot Money: Burnside and Meadows take a gamble when they set up an undercover operation in a local casino.
New. Foreign Affairs: Daniel and Joanna go on their first real date, Archie helps a macho patient deal with a dubious rash and Ronnie tells George he is filing for divorce.
New. Another Day: A difficult cancer patient falls out with Melody. Archie and Michelle play tricks on Vivien, but she goes along with the game and turns the tables on them.
Honour Thy Father: Ric and Diane are thrown together by the death of a patient, while Ed goes behind Tom's back to get a second opinion when he is told his father's heart is beyond repair.
The Enemy Within: Zoe realises she cannot keep up the pretence of her pregnancy much longer. Lenny faces a tough decision regarding the Jafa fellowship.
Hot Money: Burnside and Meadows take a gamble when they set up an undercover operation in a local casino.
New. It is Archie's funeral, and Marsden is watching the suspects like a hawk. Will the murderer slip up? A surprise guest turns up at the wake.
New. Glenda is summoned to the Vic as the Mitchells grill the surprise arrival. Meanwhile, Bradley plucks up the courage to propose to Stacey, but she has other worries on her mind.
One of Nature's Little Miracles: Fun with the vets making their mark on Yorkshire. Siegfried takes on another assistant who bizarrely brings his badger with him.
Charles learns that Avril has discharged herself from hospital. Jan considers the future of her fashion business, while Jack and Tom remain at loggerheads.
The Offered Fallacy: Frank and Lu must clear their names when they are framed for a string of crimes by some mysterious doppelgangers.
Rough Justice: Fletcher decides that Harris must be put on trial for stealing from another prisoner. You can't trust anyone.
Things to Do When Your Wife Runs off with a Turkish Waiter: More pensioner power in the much-loved sitcom. Barry and Glenda are surprised when a man they met on holiday pays a visit.
Beware of Laughing at Nora's Hats: Another slice of senior silliness. Nora reveals an ongoing feud with one Audrey Craig, who had the audacity to laugh about her hat at a wedding.
Yours Truly - If You're Not Careful: Young-at-heart sitcom. Billy and Alvin terrorise Truly with the news that the former Mrs Truelove is in town and trying to track him down.
Hidden Treasures: When Dolly complains about strange occurrences at home, Pearl is concerned about her mental well-being but sets up security cameras - leading to a surprising discovery.
To Those We Love: Pearl caters a wedding at a theme park, and when the bride found dead in the hall of mirrors she can't resist investigating - even when it leads her straight into danger.
The Sweeney meets The Likely Lads in the hit cop show. The squad investigate the death of a barrister found in his car 20 years earlier.
The Offered Fallacy: Frank and Lu must clear their names when they are framed for a string of crimes by some mysterious doppelgangers.
Sins of the Fathers: Another perplexing mystery for the chalk and cheese detectives. A village priest is found dead shortly after performing a highly-publicised miracle cure.
Trust: Tracey falls for her smooth therapist Gerald and argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship when he bans her from continuing her sessions.
I Defy Thee to Forget: The Sixth Precinct's search for the murderer of an officer is derailed as Annie creates a fissure in the Corcoran home.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Thirty Something: Sibling sitcom and the Essex girls have the birthday blues. Sharon is approaching her 30th birthday but is unable to see a bright future ahead of her.
Crash Landing: A plane makes a crash-landing in Sun Hill, plunging Daly into an investigation in which his ex-lover plays a crucial role.
New. Attack of the Centorts - Part One: A psychiatric patient runs away from hospital, convinced an alien race is on its way. Archie threatens to tell Julia about Daniel's escort activities.
New. Attack of the Centorts - Part Two: Time is running out for Mia as the past catches up with her. Nick makes a breakthrough and it's decision day for Vivien.
The Kindness of Strangers: George collapses again, so Ed pulls strings to make him a priority. Diane flirts with a locum doctor and Rosie receives legal papers outlining Sami's plans.
Nice and Easy Does It: Charlie steals drugs from the hospital as he prepares to honour Megan's wishes and end her suffering. Tess discovers what he's done and they consider other options.
Crash Landing: A plane makes a crash-landing in Sun Hill, plunging Daly into an investigation in which his ex-lover plays a crucial role.
New. There is fierce competition in the Vic as Roxy holds auditions for new bar staff, while Amira's attempt to seduce Syed leads her into dangerous territory.
New. As the new Mitchell brother causes tension, Ronnie is horrified by Jack's deception. Amira struggles to cope with her guilt, and Bianca receives an unexpected lifeline.
Barks and Bites: Baaa-rilliant comedy and drama in the historic series following vets on call in Yorkshire. Tristan and James examine a consignment of sheep for export.
Leo's disappointed to finish second in a speedboat race, and takes out his anger on the winner. Avril's haunted by nightmares about the plane crash, and Ken loses his temper with Sarah.
Nothing Will Come of Nothing: The team investigate the disappearance of a local lothario from a casino run by two eccentric sisters.
Pardon Me: Classic prison-set sitcom with the great Ronnie Barker and a guest appearance from David Jason. Fletch decides to help an old lag who has been in prison for 17 years.
The Swan Man of Ilkley: Popular sitcom that first graced our screens in 1973. Lennie from the pickle factory has visions of sailing the canals in a swan boat. Can his friends help?
Watching the Clock: Much loved sitcom. Clegg climbs a tree to prove a point and finds that what goes up doesn't always come down. Meanwhile, the police deal with a runaway cooker!
Has Anyone Seen a Peruvian Wart?: Roy Clarke's enduringly popular sitcom. Tempted by her baking, a renowned romancer of gullible women focuses his attentions on the widow Nora Batty.
Death on the Close Part 1: When a man with no ID or connection to Marlow is found dead in the middle of a suburban road, Judith, Becks and Suzie investigate.
Death on the Close Part 2: A link to a past missing persons case shines a new light on the residents of Linnet Close - but could they be responsible for murder on their doorstep?
Likely Lad James Bolam and Minder's Dennis Waterman star in another adventure for the aging detectives. The squad try to trace the attacker of an Asian woman in Southall.
Nothing Will Come of Nothing: The team investigate the disappearance of a local lothario from a casino run by two eccentric sisters.
